hops.context: In this paper, the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) algorithm is employed [6] to obtain optimal dynamic control policy for the active suspension system.
The state transition from (sµ)t to (sµ)t+1 is computed using the Quarter Car Model described in Section II, creating a continuous feedback environment for training the agent.
The state transition from (sµ)t to (sµ)t+1 is computed using the Quarter Car Model described in Section II, creating a continuous feedback environment for training the agent.

question: In the context of using DDPG for active suspension control, why is the Quarter Car Model crucial for training the Active Suspension DRL Agent?

options.A: It provides a continuous feedback environment essential for calculating state transitions and training the agent.
options.B: It provides historical data about vehicle dynamics that the DDPG algorithm can use to predict future states.
options.C: It serves as a preliminary testing environment where the DDPG algorithm is optimized before real-world implementation.
options.D: It offers a simplified version of vehicle dynamics for initial algorithm training before deploying more complex models.

explanation: The Quarter Car Model is crucial as it computes the state transition from (sµ)t to (sµ)t+1, thus creating a continuous feedback environment necessary for the DDPG-based Active Suspension DRL Agent to learn and adapt its control policies effectively.
